action_title: Deploy "timer" variant for Timer vs No Timer
description: A/B test shows timer improves quiz pass rate by 11.1% (p=0.000). Deploy to all users.

action_title: Deploy "timer" variant for Timer vs No Timer
description: A/B test shows timer improves avg session duration sec by 9.6% (p=0.009). Deploy to all users.

action_title: Deploy "notification" variant for Notification vs No Notification
description: A/B test shows notification improves days active by 15.4% (p=0.000). Deploy to all users.

action_title: Deploy "notification" variant for Notification vs No Notification
description: A/B test shows notification improves total sessions by 17.8% (p=0.000). Deploy to all users.

action_title: Address: Biggest drop-off at Absence Declared
description: 27% of users drop off at the absence declared stage. This is the #1 conversion bottleneck.

action_title: Address: Low study time → high failure rate
description: Users spending <3 minutes have 100% failure rate vs 27% for users spending more time.

action_title: Address: prof_001 rejects 1.5x more than average
description: prof_001 has 53% approval rate vs 82% average (1676 reviews). This is a potential bottleneck.

action_title: Address: prof_002 rejects 1.5x more than average
description: prof_002 has 54% approval rate vs 82% average (2295 reviews). This is a potential bottleneck.

action_title: Add progress indicators to PDF viewer
description: Users who spend more time on PDFs pass quizzes at higher rates. Adding progress tracking and time-spent indicators can increase PDF engagement by ~20%.
expected_impact: Improving PDF engagement by +20% can increase pass rate by +12%

description: Biggest retention drop occurs in first week. A guided onboarding with milestone rewards can improve D7 retention by 15-25%.
Some professors reject 2x more than average. Publishing grading rubrics and calibration sessions can normalize approval rates.
